source_units.text_raw:
The Airport Periphery Zone is established to accommodate aviation-related operations and supporting uses essential to the safe and efficient functioning of the Charlottetown Airport. The zone provides for runways, taxiways, terminals, hangars, air cargo facilities, and associated commercial and service uses, while ensuring that development remains compatible with federal aviation regulations and surrounding land uses. The intent is to protect the long-term viability of the airport as a regional transportation hub, promote economic development through aviation-related industries, and manage land use in a manner that minimizes impacts on neighbouring residential and business areas.
Rezoning from AP to another zone is not permitted.
The following uses are permitted in the AP Zone:
Transitional Housing
Commercial Daycare Centre
Cultural Establishment
Educational Institution (private)
Educational Institution (public)
Fire Station
Government Offices
Medical, Health and Dental Office
Place of Worship
Police Station
Recreation or Fitness Center (indoors)
Research and Development Facilities
Automobile Body Shop
Automobile Sales and Services
Automobile Service Station
Club
Equipment Sales, Rental Service
Garden Centre
Office
Outdoor Retail Display
Personal Service Shop
Retail Store
Retail Store with connected Retail Warehouse, Light Manufacturing or assembly facility
Retail Warehouse
Service Repair Establishment
Transportation Service
Veterinary Services
Bulk Fuel Station
Greenhouse (private industrial)
Heavy Equipment Repair
Hostel/ Hotel
Kennel
Manufacturing, Heavy
Manufacturing, Light
Marijuana Production Facility
Warehouse, Storage Facility and/or Distribution Centre
Agricultural and Resource Land Uses
Airport
Outdoor Storage
Parking Lot / Structure
Transport Terminal
These uses are permitted so long as the following lot requirements are met:
Lot Area min. 600 m 2 interior lot, 700 m 2 corner lot
Frontage min. 20 m
Front Yard Setback min. 7 .5 m
Flankage Yard Setback min. 7 .5 m
Side Yard Setback min. 3 m, 6 m if bordering on any residential zone
Rear Yard Setback min. 10 m, 20 m if bordering on any residential zone
Lot Coverage max. 35%
Accessory buildings are permitted according to Section 3.1
Notwithstanding 20.2.3, airport related facilities operated by the Charlottetown Airport Authority Inc. are permitted to include more than one building per lot, with no maximum or minimum zoning requirements.
Development in the Airport Periphery (AP) Zone may be subject to additional regulations of the Charlottetown Airport Zoning Regulations (SOR/92-649) under federal authority of the Aeronautics Act, R.S.C., 1985, c. A-2 and all amendments thereto.
Subject to Transport Canada's Obstruction Limitation Surfaces (OLS) - no building or structure may penetrate federally regulated height surfaces. Parking Lot Encouraged on side or rear, If located in front, 1 Island per 15 cars, no more than 50% of front yard area Min. Side Yard Setback 3 m; 6 m if bordering residential Min. Rear Yard Setback 10 m; 20 m if bordering residential Min. Front Yard Setback 7.5 mStorage Compounds Fenced to min. 1.8 m height Min. Frontage 20 m Max. Building Height 12 m and 3 Storeys Truck Bays - Garage Doors On Side or Rear yard Walkway Connecting Parking to Entry Figure 19.1 AP Built Form and Landscape Requirements
Permitted encroachments into the setbacks are listed in Table 3.2.
Gas stations may be permitted by Development Agreement.
All uses permitted in the Business Park (BP) Zone are permitted in the AP Zone, subject to the Regulations for Permitted Uses for the BP Zone.
Airport towers or aviation equipment that exceed the maximum height requirement are permitted as of right.
One primary building per lot is permitted.
The maximum building height shall be 12 m or 3 storeys.
All built form requirements in the BP Zone (Section 18.5) apply to all uses for new buildings in this zone, except airport related facilities.
Airport related facilities are exempt from any built for requirements such as:
Service and loading areas requirements or screening from public streets.
Garage requirements
Any parking requirements
Setback and coverage requirements
All Landscape requirements in the BP Zone (Section 18.6) apply to all uses for new buildings in this zone.
Airport related facilities are exempt from:
Any of the landscape requirements in Section 19.6
Any safety fencing requirements which prohibit the use of razor wire

The zone provides for runways, taxiways, terminals, hangars, air cargo facilities, and associated commercial and service uses, while ensuring that development remains compatible with federal aviation regulations and surrounding land uses. review_flags.description: PDF text order and figure/table text were extracted with pypdf; verify section order, column flow, and page layout before normalization.
The zone contains table, figure, or schedule text. Values are preserved as extracted text and require source PDF layout review before normalization.
